paper_title: Growth and yield estimation of Chamaecyparis spp. through tree ring analysis

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the ring width of Chamaecyparis spp. to estimate growth and yield. The analysis focuses on measuring growth parameters such as ring width, percentage of early wood and late wood widths, and ring density, which are influenced by climatic and abiotic factors.

steps.description: Collect tree ring samples from different height classes of Chamaecyparis spp.
steps.searchable_summary: collect tree ring samples from various height classes

steps.description: Measure the average radial increment of the tree in terms of ring width at different height classes.
steps.searchable_summary: measure radial increment as ring width at different heights

steps.description: Analyze the growth parameters such as ring width, percentage of early wood and late wood widths, and ring density.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze growth parameters including ring width and density

steps.description: Determine the influence of climatic and abiotic factors on tree growth.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze influence of climatic and abiotic factors on growth
outputs: influence analysis of climatic and abiotic factors

steps.description: Compare the growth rates of C. obstusa and C. pisifera species.
steps.searchable_summary: compare growth rates of C. obstusa and C. pisifera

steps.description: Visualize the growth trends over time, highlighting periods of faster and slower growth.
